title: cyathea chiricana
intro: Cyathea chiricana, also known as the Chiricahua Tree Fern, is a type of evergreen tree fern native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Cyathea chiricana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and provide antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to make teas, salves, and oils for topical and internal applications.
